name: clustr
description: Manage your Proxmox VE cluster (nodes, VMs, LXC containers) from Claude.
long_description: Clustr exposes your Proxmox VE infrastructure as MCP tools so you can inspect and manage nodes, VMs, and LXC containers directly from Claude. Runs locally over stdio: no network port is opened. It can only do what the Proxmox API token you provide is allowed to do; use a least-privilege token (PVEAuditor for read-only).

proxmox_host.title: Proxmox Host
proxmox_host.description: IP or hostname of your Proxmox node (e.g. 192.168.1.10). Optional: you can leave every field below blank, install, then ask Claude to 'set up Clustr for <your host IP>' to generate a correctly-scoped token, and paste it back here.

proxmox_user.title: Proxmox User
proxmox_user.description: Proxmox user with the realm, e.g. root@pam (or clustr@pve if you used the streamlined setup).

proxmox_token_name.title: API Token Name
proxmox_token_name.description: The API token ID you created in Proxmox (Datacenter → API Tokens). Don't have one yet? Leave blank, install, and ask Claude to 'set up Clustr for <your host IP>'.

proxmox_token_value.title: API Token Secret
proxmox_token_value.description: The token secret value. Stored securely in your OS keychain.

proxmox_verify_ssl.title: Verify TLS certificate
proxmox_verify_ssl.description: Leave off for Proxmox's default self-signed certificate.

extra_endpoints_json.title: Additional clusters (advanced, optional)
extra_endpoints_json.description: Manage more than one Proxmox cluster: a JSON array of extra endpoints, e.g. [{"name":"office","host":"10.0.0.5","tokenName":"clustr","tokenValue":"…"}]. Target one with the 'host' argument on any tool. The host above stays your 'default'.

endpoints_file.title: Endpoints file (advanced, optional)
endpoints_file.description: Path to a writable JSON file where add_endpoint / remove_endpoint persist runtime changes (so they survive restarts), e.g. C:\\Users\\you\\clustr-endpoints.json.
